- The 'Security' tab will display more code scanning analysis results (e.g., for the default branch).
- Depending on your configuration and choice of analysis tool, future pull requests will be annotated with code scanning analysis results.
- You will be able to see the analysis results for the pull request's branch on this [overview](/LFDT-Lockness/generic-ecies/security/code-scanning?query=pr%3A18+is%3Aopen) once the scans have completed and the checks have passed.

issue.body:
### Organization / team

Workflow smoke test

### Contract type

Statement of Work (SOW)

### Business context

Public-style RevOps implementation and managed services sample. This is not a real client document.

### Main concerns

- [X] Payment timing or acceptance gates
- [X] Scope creep or change orders
- [X] Renewal, termination, or non-renewal

### Redacted excerpt or public-style sample

Vendor will provide onboarding, dashboard configuration, automation support, and additional support as requested by Customer. Customer will pay invoices on Net 90 terms after acceptance by the Customer project owner. Acceptance may be delayed at Customer's sole discretion until all internal stakeholders approve deliverables. The engagement renews month-to-month after launch and Customer may terminate for convenience with 30 days notice.

### Acknowledgement

- [X] I understand this is business-risk review, not legal advice.
- [X] I have not posted confidential information, personal data, passwords, or private client documents.

review.body:
Traced the control flow end to end and didn't find a hard blocker. Things I specifically verified: the `--allow-remote-scan` gating holds for object stores (full download routes through `materialize_input` → `require_remote_scan_allowed` before any network; summary/range reads stay opt-in-free, matching HTTP); URL redaction strips query/userinfo on every object-store error path; the advertised scheme list (`s3/s3a/gs/az/adl/azure/abfs/abfss`) is exactly what `object_store`'s `ObjectStoreScheme::parse` recognizes; `https://…amazonaws.com`/Azure hosts still go through the existing ureq path since `is_http_url` matches first; and range/seek bounds are correct. `main` is sync, so the per-source `block_on` won't hit a nested-runtime panic.

Three inline notes (one suggestion on tokio features, one panic-avoidance on `env::vars()`, one design question on streaming vs. full materialization). None are merge-blocking.

Two things for the body:

- **Test coverage gap.** Every test drives the in-memory store via `new_for_test`, which bypasses `ObjectStoreSource::open` entirely — so the actual `Url::parse` → `parse_url_opts` → store construction and the env-var credential plumbing have zero coverage. I get that hitting real S3/GCS/Azure in CI is impractical, but right now the part most likely to break in the field (scheme routing + credential sourcing) is the untested part. Worth at least a unit test on `ObjectStoreSource::open` for the parse-error/redaction paths.

- **Docs.** `rust/cli/README.md` pre-1.0 item 7 ("Future object-store remote input policy") describes exactly what this PR ships — update it so shipped behavior isn't still listed as future work. More importantly for users: nothing documents *which schemes are supported* or *how credentials are sourced*. Someone running `mcap cat s3://…` has no way to discover they need `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` etc. in the environment (or `AWS_SKIP_SIGNATURE=true` for public buckets). A short "Remote inputs" section listing the schemes and noting that auth comes from standard env vars / default credential chains would save a lot of "why does this 403" confusion.

Draft PR, so assuming docs/tests are still in flight — flagging now so they're on the radar.

issue.title: Repository: Update .gitignore with common local development file patterns
issue.body:
## Summary
Update the existing .gitignore file to include common local development file patterns that are currently not excluded, preventing accidental commits of local environment files and temporary artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Add patterns for common local environment files:
  - .env.local
  - .env.*.local
  - *.log
  - *.tmp
  - *.temp
- AC-2: Ensure no existing valid files are accidentally excluded by the new patterns
- AC-3: Keep the .gitignore file organized with clear section headers for new additions

issue.title: PIT Stage 12 Slice 1 - Runtime Shell and Route Foundation
issue.body:
Purpose: begin PIT Stage 12 implementation from merged PR #1768. This issue covers the first runtime implementation slice only and does not claim Stage 12 completion or FUNCTIONAL_PASS.

Authority: PR #1768 merged; issue #1767 authorizes Stage 12; appointed builder is pit-specialist; Build Authorization is cleared; binding RED baseline is 147 tests including PIT-RED-ROUTE-029, PIT-RED-TIMELINE-011, and PIT-RED-TIMELINE-012.

Scope: confirm the PIT runtime surface, scaffold W8.1 PIT route foundations, implement public/private route boundaries, implement app shell/layout, implement unauthenticated protected-route baseline, implement global error boundary and 404 foundation, add initial route and shell tests, and file governed Stage 12 slice evidence.

Non-scope: no full module completion claim, no FUNCTIONAL_PASS claim, no deployed LFV completion claim, no CS2 L3 closure claim, and no broad business-domain implementation beyond shell/routing foundation.

Required evidence: implementation code, tests and honest status, known gaps, scope declaration, builder appointment/reference, ECAP before handover, IAA review before handover, Foreman QP, and CI/status summary.

release.name: 1.0.0 - Mambo Desktop slug (Andy convention)
release.body:
## Mambo Desktop for Admin2 1.0.0

### Breaking slug rename (Andy / Grav convention)
- **Folder / GPM slug:** `mambo-desktop-admin2` (was `grav-desktop-admin2`)
- **API routes:** `/mambo-desktop/*` (was `/grav-desktop/*`)
- **Repo:** `grav-plugin-mambo-desktop-admin2`

### Upgrade
1. Disable/remove `grav-desktop-admin2`
2. Install `mambo-desktop-admin2` (this zip)
3. Optional: plugin auto-renames `user/data/grav-desktop-admin2/` → `user/data/mambo-desktop-admin2/` on first run
4. `bin/grav cache` — open **Mambo Desktop** in Admin2

### Includes Phases A/B/C
Sticky notes, operator tools, wallpaper strip, Team DC Arcade support, window manager.

**Arcade games:** `mambo-desktop-arcade-bundle.zip` (separate asset on release page).

issue.title: feat(workflows): hide generic schedule trigger from selector
issue.body:
## Problem

The generic "schedule" trigger type in workflows has no compelling use case right now and is being used in ways it wasn't designed for, generating support noise. We want to hide it until it becomes useful again (e.g. once we have an LLM node or similar), while keeping the **batch** trigger — including recurring batch scheduling — fully intact.

## Changes

- Hide the generic **Schedule** trigger from the trigger-type dropdown for new workflows.
- The option is still included in the dropdown **only when the current trigger is already a schedule**, so existing schedule-based workflows open without crashing, show their configured trigger, and can be switched to a different trigger type.
- This mirrors the existing conditional-inclusion pattern already used for the legacy `manual` trigger in the same component (`StepTrigger.tsx`).
- The **Batch** trigger and its recurring `RecurringSchedulePicker` schedule are untouched.
- Backend validation, the schedule status badge, and the step view rendering for schedule triggers are left in place so already-configured workflows continue to behave correctly.

### Production usage of the generic schedule trigger

The trigger config lives in the application Postgres (`posthog_hogflow.trigger`), which isn't reachable from the analytics tooling I had access to, so I couldn't run the count myself. For whoever has DB access, the count of distinct workflows currently using it is:

```sql
SELECT count(DISTINCT id)
FROM posthog_hogflow
WHERE trigger->>'type' = 'schedule';
```

(rows are versioned by `(team, version, id)`, hence `DISTINCT id`).

## How did you test this code?

I'm an agent. I did not perform manual browser testing. No automated tests covered the dropdown option list, and the change is a UI-only conditional that follows an existing, tested pattern (`manual`). Existing schedule-trigger logic/validation tests (`workflowLogic.schedule.test.ts`, `types.test.ts`) are unaffected by this change.

## Automatic notifications

- [ ] Publish to changelog?
- [ ] Alert Sales and Marketing teams?

## Docs update

No docs change needed.

## 🤖 Agent context

Authored by PostHog Code (Claude Opus 4.8) from a Slack request to hide the generic schedule trigger while preserving the batch trigger and not breaking existing schedule workflows.

Approach: an `Explore` sub-agent mapped the workflows trigger system (Zod schemas in `steps/types.ts`, the trigger registry, `StepTrigger.tsx` selector, backend serializer/validation, and the `HogFlowSchedule` model). The selector hardcodes each built-in trigger option, and `manual` was already conditionally shown only when active — I reused that exact pattern for `schedule` rather than fully deleting the option, because the requirement is to hide it for new flows while keeping existing flows openable and switchable. Removing the `handleSelect`/render/validation branches was rejected since they're still needed to render and migrate existing schedule workflows.

---
*Created with [PostHog Code](https://posthog.com/code?ref=pr)*

comment.body:
<!-- author-email-nudge -->
Hey @dmarchuk! 👋

It looks like your git author email on this PR isn't your `@posthog.com` address (`dmarchuk@me.com`). Since you're on the PostHog team, it's worth pointing your local git author email at your `@posthog.com` address. Why it matters:

- **Consistent work identity in git history** — internal tooling that attributes commits to team members keys off your `@posthog.com` address.
- **Keeps team contributions easy to tell apart from external community ones** when scanning history.

You can fix it for this repo with:

```bash
git config user.email "you@posthog.com"
```

Or set it globally with `git config --global user.email "you@posthog.com"`. No need to redo this PR — just a nudge for next time. 🙂

issue.title: [SIKESRA][SEQ-12][P2] Integrate SIKESRA RBAC/ABAC with EmDash user role assignment
issue.body:
## Problem

SIKESRA access control and ABAC configuration must be isolated from EmDash core and other plugins. Production access policies must be queryable, auditable, and stored in dedicated D1 tables with the `sikesra_` prefix.

However, SIKESRA must not create a completely separate user system. The SIKESRA role and ABAC layer must be based on EmDash users and the existing EmDash RBAC/user role management model, so SIKESRA roles can be assigned to actual EmDash users from the admin user management flow.

The current EmDash admin user detail panel already supports role assignment such as `Pelanggan`, `Contributor`, `Penulis`, `Editor`, and `Admin`. SIKESRA must extend or map into this user/role basis safely without modifying EmDash core.

## Goal

Create SIKESRA RBAC and ABAC configuration using dedicated `sikesra_` D1 tables, while integrating with EmDash users and EmDash's existing RBAC concept so SIKESRA roles/scopes can be assigned to EmDash users.

## Required Tables

```txt
sikesra_permission_catalog
sikesra_role_catalog
sikesra_role_permission_assignments
sikesra_user_role_assignments
sikesra_user_scope_assignments
sikesra_abac_attribute_catalog
sikesra_abac_subject_assignments
sikesra_abac_resource_assignments
sikesra_abac_policy_rules
```

## EmDash User Integration Rule

SIKESRA must use EmDash user identity as the source of user identity.

Required behavior:

- SIKESRA must reference EmDash user IDs, not create duplicate SIKESRA-only users.
- SIKESRA role assignment must be attachable to existing EmDash users.
- EmDash global roles such as Admin/Editor/etc. may be mapped to SIKESRA capabilities, but SIKESRA-specific operational roles must be stored separately in `sikesra_` tables.
- SIKESRA must not modify EmDash core user tables directly.
- SIKESRA may read trusted EmDash user/session identity through supported EmDash APIs/context.
- SIKESRA must not trust client-provided user headers as production identity.

## Required SIKESRA Roles

Create a SIKESRA role catalog that can be assigned to EmDash users:

```txt
sikesra_admin
sikesra_operator_kabupaten
sikesra_verifikator_kabupaten
sikesra_verifikator_sopd
sikesra_verifikator_kecamatan
sikesra_verifikator_desa_kelurahan
sikesra_operator_desa_kelurahan
sikesra_viewer_laporan
sikesra_viewer_publikasi
sikesra_auditor
```

## Required SIKESRA Permission Catalog

At minimum:

```txt
sikesra.dashboard.read
sikesra.registry.read
sikesra.registry.create
sikesra.registry.update
sikesra.registry.delete_soft
sikesra.registry.read_sensitive
sikesra.document.read
sikesra.document.upload
sikesra.document.read_restricted
sikesra.import.create
sikesra.import.validate
sikesra.import.promote
sikesra.verification.read
sikesra.verification.approve
sikesra.verification.reject
sikesra.report.read
sikesra.export.create
sikesra.export.restricted
sikesra.audit.read
sikesra.settings.read
sikesra.settings.update
sikesra.rbac.manage
sikesra.abac.manage
```

## Required User Assignment Model

SIKESRA assignment must support:

```txt
emdash_user_id
sikesra_role_slug
tenant_id
site_id
region_scope_type
region_scope_code
organization_scope_type
organization_scope_code
is_active
valid_from
valid_until
created_at
updated_at
deleted_at
created_by
updated_by
```

Recommended tables:

```txt
sikesra_user_role_assignments
sikesra_user_scope_assignments
```

## ABAC Scope Requirements

SIKESRA ABAC must support region and organization scoping for EmDash users.

Required region scope types:

```txt
all
province
regency
district
village
custom_region
```

Required organization scope examples:

```txt
kabupaten_admin
sopd
kecamatan
desa_kelurahan
lks
lembaga_keagamaan
pendidikan_keagamaan
```

Example mappings:

- `sikesra_admin`: all regions, all modules.
- `sikesra_verifikator_kabupaten`: one regency, verification and report access.
- `sikesra_verifikator_sopd`: one or more SOPD/org scopes, specific verification queue.
- `sikesra_verifikator_kecamatan`: one district, limited verification queue.
- `sikesra_verifikator_desa_kelurahan`: one village, village-level data entry and verification.
- `sikesra_auditor`: read audit/report only, no mutation.

## Admin UI Requirements

Add or update SIKESRA admin UI so operators can manage SIKESRA access for EmDash users.

Required pages/components:

```txt
/access/roles
/access/permissions
/access/matrix
/access/users
/access/scopes
/access/preview
/abac/attributes
/abac/policies
/abac/preview
```

Required UI behavior:

- List EmDash users or search/select an EmDash user.
- Assign one or more SIKESRA roles to an EmDash user.
- Assign region scope to the user.
- Assign organization/SOPD scope to the user where needed.
- Show effective permissions.
- Show effective ABAC decision preview.
- Allow deactivation of SIKESRA role assignment without deleting the EmDash user.
- Audit every role/scope/ABAC change.

## Runtime Enforcement Requirements

All non-public SIKESRA routes must enforce:

1. trusted EmDash authenticated user identity;
2. SIKESRA role assignment;
3. SIKESRA permission;
4. ABAC region scope;
5. ABAC organization/module scope;
6. data sensitivity/masking rules;
7. audit logging for sensitive or mutating action.

Example route checks:

```txt
registry/list -> sikesra.registry.read + region scope filter
registry/save -> sikesra.registry.create/update + region scope check
verification/advance -> sikesra.verification.approve + current verification level + region/org scope
settings/save -> sikesra.settings.update
access/* -> sikesra.rbac.manage
abac/* -> sikesra.abac.manage
export/* -> sikesra.export.create or sikesra.export.restricted
```

## Tasks

- Create D1 schema for SIKESRA permission catalog.
- Create D1 schema for SIKESRA role catalog.
- Create D1 schema for role-permission assignments.
- Create D1 schema for EmDash user to SIKESRA role assignments.
- Create D1 schema for EmDash user region/org scope assignments.
- Create D1 schema for ABAC attributes, subjects, resources, and policy rules.
- Refactor access preview and ABAC preview routes to use D1.
- Integrate SIKESRA assignments with trusted EmDash user identity.
- Add admin UI to assign SIKESRA roles/scopes to EmDash users.
- Ensure explicit deny overrides allow.
- Add audit events for access/ABAC changes.
- Add tests for EmDash user assignment, permission preview, ABAC preview, and route enforcement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Access control data uses D1 tables with `sikesra_` prefix.
- ABAC data uses D1 tables with `sikesra_` prefix.
- SIKESRA roles can be assigned to existing EmDash users.
- SIKESRA does not create duplicate user accounts.
- SIKESRA does not modify EmDash core user tables.
- User assignment supports tenant, site, region, and organization scope.
- Effective permissions can be previewed for an EmDash user.
- Effective ABAC decision can be previewed for an EmDash user.
- Non-public SIKESRA routes enforce SIKESRA RBAC/ABAC using trusted EmDash user identity.
- Explicit deny overrides allow.
- Tests cover permission preview, ABAC preview, EmDash user role assignment, scope assignment, and route denial.
- No EmDash core table is modified.

## Validation

```bash
cd awcmsmicro-dev/packages/plugins/awcms-micro-sikesra
pnpm typecheck
pnpm test
pnpm build
```

## Architectural Rule

SIKESRA must use EmDash users as the source of identity, but SIKESRA-specific roles, permissions, scopes, and ABAC policies must stay isolated in `sikesra_` D1 tables inside the AWCMS-Micro SIKESRA plugin. Do not modify EmDash core.

performed_via_github_app.name: ChatGPT Codex Connector
performed_via_github_app.description: Bring ChatGPT and Codex to your GitHub repositories.

comment.body:
Issue #145 is now closed, so the production identity blocker for this RBAC/ABAC issue is removed.

Available foundation from #145:
- private plugin API route passes trusted EmDash locals.user into plugin route context
- RouteContext.user / SandboxedRouteContext.user are available to plugin handlers
- SIKESRA getRequestUserId(ctx) prefers ctx.user.id and only falls back to X-Sikesra-User-Id in non-production dev/test contexts
- regression test covers spoofed SIKESRA headers losing to trusted context identity
- downstream core changes are captured in awcmsmicro-dev/.awcms-patches/0021-plugin-route-user-context.patch and documented in the divergence log

Next #132 work can focus on closing the remaining role/scope/ABAC assignment acceptance criteria without relying on production-trusted client headers.

issue.title: UI — referral link generation + reward status
[app A0] Gate: endpoints backend para o app mobile (árvore + agregação + auth JWT + exposição remota)
issue.body:
## Gate pré-condição do app Cockpit i9

> **Blocker.** O Flutter app não faz nada útil sem estes 4 contratos de backend. Implementar antes de qualquer fase A1+.

### 1 — Endpoint de árvore `GET /api/app/tree`

Retorna hierarquia completa da frota:
```json
{
  "projects": [{
    "id": "i9-team",
    "teams": [{
      "id": "dev",
      "agents": [
        { "id": "orchestrator", "status": "working", "focus": "...", "cost_usd": 0.42, "hibernating": false }
      ]
    }]
  }]
}
```
Fonte: `teams.json` + bridge cross-machine (RelayWorker) + agent-context atual.

### 2 — Endpoints de agregação de status

```
GET /api/app/projects/:project/summary
→ { total_agents, online, working, hibernating, total_cost_usd }

GET /api/app/teams/:project/:team/summary
→ idem, escopo do team
```
Alimenta cards de nível 1 (projetos) e nível 2 (teams) na navegação do app.

### 3 — Auth mobile JWT `POST /api/app/auth`

```
POST /api/app/auth
Body:     { "secret": "<token-compartilhado>" }
Response: { "token": "<JWT>", "expires_in": 86400 }
```
- JWT assinado com `APP_JWT_SECRET` (armazenado em i9-secrets, namespace `ambientes/`)
- Auth simplificado pra uso interno (sem OAuth externo)
- Token expira em 24h; app faz refresh automático
- Middleware de authn nos endpoints `/api/app/*` valida Bearer token

### 4 — Exposição remota segura

O app Flutter precisa acessar o backend (porta 7680) **de fora da rede local** (celular/iPad do Lee em qualquer lugar).

Opções (avaliar e decidir):
- [ ] **Tailscale** *(recomendado)* — VPN mesh zero-config; `ubuntu-claude-agents` + device Lee. Zero infra nova.
- [ ] **Cloudflare Tunnel** — `cloudflared tunnel` → localhost:7680; URL pública + auth opcional.
- [ ] **Proxy reverso TLS** — nginx + Let's Encrypt + authn (mais complexo).
- [ ] **ngrok** — dev/temporário, não recomendado pra uso contínuo.

**Ação**: decidir opção + configurar + documentar em `ambientes/ubuntu-claude-agents`.

### DoD

- [ ] `GET /api/app/tree` retornando hierarquia real (cross-machine incluso)
- [ ] `GET /api/app/projects/:p/summary` e `GET /api/app/teams/:p/:t/summary` funcionais
- [ ] `POST /api/app/auth` retornando JWT válido; middleware validando Bearer em `/api/app/*`
- [ ] `APP_JWT_SECRET` no i9-secrets (`ambientes/`), nunca hardcoded
- [ ] Opção de exposição remota decidida, configurada e documentada no vault
- [ ] Smoke: simulator Flutter acessa backend via URL remota + auth OK

**Depende de:** headless #146 (runner ativo para status real)  
**Bloqueia:** #APP-A2 (auth), #APP-A3 (navegação)

**Dono:** dev-backend

issue.title: sk-model-resolver unreachable via Skill tool; orchestration skills duplicate slash menu
issue.body:
## Problem

Two related skill-frontmatter defects surfaced during a `running-a-pipeline` run.

### 1. `sk-model-resolver` is unreachable (runtime bug)

`skills/sk-model-resolver/SKILL.md` declares `disable-model-invocation: true`, which blocks model-initiated `Skill` tool loads. But `running-a-pipeline` Phase 0.45 "Full Path" (`skills/running-a-pipeline/SKILL.md:144`) instructs: *"Load `sk-model-resolver` via the `Skill` tool."* Result:

```
Error: Skill superpipelines:sk-model-resolver cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation
```

The flag pair (`disable-model-invocation: true` + `user-invocable: false`) is the `{agent}-protocol` skill convention per `CLAUDE.md` File-Layout Rules. `sk-model-resolver` is a utility skill loaded programmatically by another skill — not an agent protocol skill — so it should not carry `disable-model-invocation`.

### 2. Orchestration skills duplicate the slash menu (surface hygiene)

`running-a-pipeline`, `creating-a-pipeline`, `adding/updating/deleting-a-pipeline-step`, and `change-models` each have a thin `commands/*.md` wrapper (the intended public `/` entry) but lack `user-invocable: false`, so they also appear directly in the slash menu, duplicating the entry.

## Fix

- Step 1: remove `disable-model-invocation: true` from `sk-model-resolver` (keep `user-invocable: false`).
- Step 2: add `user-invocable: false` to the 6 command-wrapped orchestration skills.
- Docs: capture the invocation-flag trap in `AIBOARDING.md`.

## Related follow-up

`sk-pipeline-grilling` carries the same `disable-model-invocation: true` while being orchestrator-loaded by `creating-a-pipeline` — likely the same latent bug. Out of scope here; tracking separately.

issue.title: feat: [RBAC] support user description in credentials

labels.name: kind/feature
labels.description: Issues related to feature request from users

labels.name: area/dependency
labels.description: Pull requests that update a dependency file

labels.name: size/XXL
labels.description: Denotes a PR that changes 1000+ lines.

issue.body:
- Wire credential descriptions through create/update/read paths, including proxy and HTTP v2 request handling, length validation, optional update presence handling, and internal credential proto/model persistence.
- Preserve existing encrypted passwords and descriptions during RootCoord credential updates when either field is omitted, reject malformed direct password updates, and keep sha256 passwords cache-only.
- Skip auth-cache refresh for description-only credential updates so existing authentication state is not blanked, while still refreshing caches for password updates.
- Return persisted user descriptions from select_user and HTTP v2 describe_user, including include_role_info=false, and fail user reads if the credential row cannot be loaded.

issue.title: Design capsule data model and access rules
issue.body:
We need a solid foundation for how capsules work before building more features.

Please plan and implement the core data model and access rules for capsules:

what fields a capsule should have
locked vs opened state
private vs public visibility
ownership rules
open date behavior
any related constraints needed for future media support
Start with a short implementation plan first, then review the plan, then implement it.

This should result in a clean schema and clear backend rules that later issues can build on.

 **Acceptance:**

capsule model supports the MVP cleanly
locked/opened behavior is clearly defined
private/public access rules are enforced
schema or migrations are added
implementation is documented briefly in the PR
